nodes.label: Rule 1: pyarud decides after reconciliation
nodes.rationale: Before applying the rule that pyarud wins in a disagreement, the system attempts to reconcile case-ending flags programmatically to avoid false-positive disagreements on poetry license.

nodes.label: Rule 2: Locked Verses
nodes.rationale: Once a verse passes pyarud, it is locked. Locked verses are never resubmitted to the diacritizer to avoid the risk of degradation on subsequent passes.

nodes.label: Rule 3: Verification Code is Read-Only
nodes.rationale: No subagent or custom tool can edit validation logic to prevent agents from hacking or bypassing checkers.

nodes.label: Rule 4: Dataset Writes via commit_verse Only
nodes.rationale: Dataset writes must go through a dedicated tool that runs a validation check before write, avoiding unsafe direct filesystem edits.

nodes.label: Rule 5: Max 3 Correction Passes
nodes.rationale: To prevent infinite loops and forcing incorrect inputs, correction retries are capped at 3 passes, after which they are logged as unresolved.

nodes.label: Rule 6: tanwīn fatḥ bug on alif maqṣūra
nodes.rationale: pyarud miscounts syllables on words ending with tanwin fath on an alif maqsura, requiring phonetic normalization and metadata flags.
